preamble: The shift from a nasal [n] to a lateral [l] is a form of denasalization or lateralization. This is frequently observed in East Asian languages and occasionally in Romance languages through liquid-nasal confusion.

examples.note: In certain phonological positions or historical stages, initial [n] has shifted to [l] or been dropped (n-initial law).
Historical confusion and shift between liquids and nasals in specific lexemes.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
